imports cheaper, reducing export revenues and increasing import spending. This causes a reduction
inthe current account surplus.

inthe current account surplus.

reduce the surplus. At the same time, appreciation lowers inflation by reducing the cost of imported
goods, offsettingCanada’s higher inflation relative to theUS.

problems.answer_value: Thereal interest ratewill increase; theprivate investment spending willdecrease.

Public Savings = Tax Revenues – Government Spending – Government Transfer
Payments = 400 – 300 – 210 = -110. National Savings = Private Savings + Public Savings = 300 +

loanablefunds, pushingreal interest rates upand causing crowding out ofprivate investment.

problems.statement:

I.Interest rates and bondprices typically have aninverse relationship.
II.The zero lower bound refers to the central bank’s inability to set the real interest rate to below
zero.
III.Quantitative easing involves the central bank selling bonds and securities to increase the money
supply.Interest rates cannot be set ina currency union.
problems.solution_text:

existingbonds (with lower fixed returns)become less valuable, so theirprices fall.

belowzero (real interest rates can be negative ifinflation is high).

money intothe economy (selling bonds reduces money supply, a contractionary policy).

4. The zero lower bound refers to the central bank’s inability to set nominal interest rates
belowzero (real interest rates can be negative ifinflation is high).
5. III: Wrong.
solution_stages.full_calculation:
I: Correct. Interest rates and bond prices have an inverse relationship—when interest rates rise,
existingbonds (with lower fixed returns)become less valuable, so theirprices fall.

belowzero (real interest rates can be negative ifinflation is high).

money intothe economy (selling bonds reduces money supply, a contractionary policy).

problems.display_name: LSESU Economics Challenge - Sample Test - Micro
problems.statement: Marginal costis equal to average variable costand average total cost when

choices.A: Marginal cost intersects average variable cost and average total cost at their maximum points.
choices.B: Average total cost and average variable cost intersect marginal cost at its maximum point.
choices.C: Marginal cost intersects average variable cost and average total cost at their minimum points.
choices.D: Average total cost and average variable cost intersect marginal cost at its minimum point.

problems.answer_value: Marginal cost intersects average variable cost and average total cost at their minimum points.
problems.solution_text:
The marginal cost (MC) curve always intersects both the average variable cost (AVC)
and average total cost (ATC) curves at their lowest points. When MC is belowAVC orATC, it pulls
them down. When MC is above AVC or ATC, it pushes them up. Therefore, the point where MC
equalsAVC orATC marks the minimum of thosecurves —where average costs stop falling and start
rising.
